name: Walk through the Turia Gardens
name_original: Paseo por los Jardines del Turia
region_intro: Touring cycling in Valencia benefits from the region's flat terrain and extensive network of dedicated cycle paths. The landscape features a blend of urban green spaces, such as the Turia Gardens, and significant natural areas like the Albufera Natural Park with its freshwater lake and rice fields. Coastal routes offer views of the Mediterranean Sea, while repurposed railway lines provide additional cycling opportunities. The region's diverse topography includes both flat coastal plains and, further inland, hills and mountainous backdrops like Sierra Calderona, offering varied elevation gains. Best touring cycling routes in Valencia The most popular touring cycling route is Valencia - El Saler - Sueca Trail, an 18.2 miles (29.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This easy route explores coastal areas and wetlands south of the city. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Walk through the Turia Gardens, an easy 12.1 miles (19.5 km) path. This route follows the car-free urban park, connecting various city attractions. Local touring cyclists also love the Albufera Loop Trail, a 27.8 miles (44.8 km) trail leading through the Albufera Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Touring cycling in Valencia is defined by urban greenways like the Turia Gardens, coastal paths, and the unique wetlands of Albufera Natural Park. The network offers options for various ability levels, from flat city rides to routes with moderate elevation gains. The routes in Valencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 6,000 reviews. More than 69,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Valencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Valencia?
A: Valencia offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 3,000 options recorded on komoot. These routes cater to all skill levels, from easy urban rides to more challenging trails in the surrounding mountains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Valencia?
A: Valencia's terrain is quite diverse. You'll find extensive flat areas, especially within the city and along the coast, perfect for leisurely rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e916384851/walk-through-the-turia-gardens" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turia Gardens&lt;/a&gt; offer a car-free urban environment, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e916534313/albufera-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; features flat wetlands and rice fields. For those seeking more challenge, the region also includes mountainous backdrops like Sierra Calderona and Sierra de Espadán, offering routes with significant elevation gains.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Valencia?
A: Valencia boasts a pleasant climate year-round, making it suitable for touring cycling in most seasons. The mild winters and warm, sunny springs and autumns are particularly ideal. Even in summer, coastal routes and shaded paths like those in the Turia Gardens or El Saler Forest offer comfortable riding conditions.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Valencia?
A: Yes, Valencia is very family-friendly for cyclists. The city's flat terrain and dedicated cycle paths, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e916384851/walk-through-the-turia-gardens" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turia Gardens&lt;/a&gt;, are ideal for beginners and families. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e916513635/valencia-el-saler-sueca-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valencia - El Saler - Sueca Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offer easy, flat exploration of coastal areas and wetlands.

Q: Are there more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. While many routes are flat, experienced cyclists can find more challenging options, particularly when venturing inland towards the mountain ranges. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918419688/via-verde-de-ojos-negros-camino-del-cid-from-sagunto-to-segorbe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Ojos Negros & Camino del Cid — From Sagunto to Segorbe&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains and longer distances, providing a more demanding experience through varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Valencia?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e916534313/albufera-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the stunning Albufera Natural Park. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23850995" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pujol Pond – Queen Sofía Palace of the Arts loop from Aragó&lt;/a&gt;, offering a mix of natural beauty and urban landmarks.

Q: What natural parks can I explore by bike in Valencia?
A: Valencia is home to several natural parks perfect for cycling. The most prominent is the &lt;a href="https://www.visitvalencia.com/en/what-to-see-valencia/albufera-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, known for its large freshwater lake, rice fields, and birdwatching opportunities. Further inland, the Sierra Calderona and Sierra de Espadán natural parks offer more rugged terrain and beautiful pine and cork forests for adventurous cyclists.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by touring bike in Valencia?
A: While not directly on the main touring cycling routes, the Valencia region does feature beautiful waterfalls that can be visited as part of a cycling trip or a short detour. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/331736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turche Cave Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vikings' Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. These natural attractions often require a short walk from the nearest accessible point.

Q: What cultural landmarks or attractions can I visit along touring cycling routes?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Valencia pass by significant landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e916384851/walk-through-the-turia-gardens" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turia Gardens&lt;/a&gt; route takes you past the modern &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23850995" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City of Arts and Sciences&lt;/a&gt;. You can also cycle to the historic center to see the Cathedral, Central Market, and ancient towers. For a blend of history and nature, consider a route towards Sagunto, an ancient city with a Roman theater.

Q: What are Vías Verdes and are there any in Valencia?
A: Vías Verdes (Greenways) are disused railway lines converted into cycling and walking paths, offering flat, car-free routes through scenic landscapes. Valencia is a starting point for such trails, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e917419564/via-verde-de-xurra-and-coastal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de Xurra&lt;/a&gt;, which passes through olive and orange groves, combining exercise, nature, and history.

Q: How can I get to the touring cycling routes using public transport in Valencia?
A: Valencia's public transport system, including metro, tram, and bus, generally accommodates bicycles, though specific rules and peak hour restrictions may apply. For detailed information on carrying bikes on public transport, it's best to consult the official EMT (bus) and Metrovalencia websites, or the general cycling information on &lt;a href="https://www.visitvalencia.com/en/what-to-do-valencia/valencia-by-bike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit Valencia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car if I want to cycle outside the city center?
A: Many of Valencia's touring cycling routes start or pass through areas with public parking facilities. For routes venturing into natural parks or rural areas, look for designated parking lots near trailheads or in nearby towns. For example, when exploring Albufera, you can often find parking in towns like El Palmar or near specific access points to the park.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Valencia?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 6,000 reviews. Reviewers often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e916384851/walk-through-the-turia-gardens" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turia Gardens&lt;/a&gt; to the unique wetlands of Albufera, and the well-maintained cycle paths that make exploration enjoyable for all skill levels.
tips: This bridge dates from the sixteenth century. A large water feature under the bridge is a reminder of the time when Turia Park was still a river and the many palm trees around the bridge immediately give you that wonderful holiday feeling. Nowadays, the Puente del Mar is only accessible to pedestrians. Are you in Valencia during the winter months? Because of the slightly higher location of this bridge, you can enjoy a lovely sun in one of the seating areas later in the day.

One of the modernist icons of the city of Valencia, the set of unique buildings in their design is dedicated to scientific and cultural dissemination. It is made up of six large buildings, the Hemisfèric, the Umbracle, the Museu de les Ciències, the Oceanogràfic (being the largest aquarium in Europe), the Palau de les Arts Reina Sofía, and the Ágora.
It is a real pleasure at any time of the year, walking all its internal paths between the buildings and being able to appreciate every detail of the architecture is a privilege for those who walk here.
Undoubtedly, the architecture and the impressive constructions stand out in its beautiful natural environment. The work of two Spanish architects of international prestige, Santiago Calatrava and Félix Candela, have given life to this world-renowned icon of the city.

Another of the current icons of the city of Valencia, this beautiful zoo is enjoyed and admired by people of all ages. It is an immersive experience that will take you through various animals, each living in a recreated area just like their natural habitat. It is undoubtedly a place to visit and spend a nice day with the whole family.

The Serranos towers are, together with the Quart portal, the only old entrances to the wall of Valencia that remain standing. The opulence of its architecture shows more of a symbolic character than a defensive one. Built in the 14th century, they were intended to show everyone who entered the city how important Valencia was in the Middle Ages.
